texto: On becoming a mother, I had forever left that solitary state of girlhood behind, and if I sometimes pined to be alone with Augustine as we used to be in our first love, a quick glance at my sleeping son's face soon banished such foolish thoughts. It was as if Augustine and I had been in a beautiful bubble but when my body split open in childbirth, the shimmering membrane broke and we were delivered to the world.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Suzanne M. Wolfe: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “On becoming a mother, I had forever left that solitary state of girlhood behind, and if I sometim…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que familia, community, motherhood pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de motherhood.
